axisWeights.$comment: Weights of the 4 delivery axes inside the headline. Change dominates at 0.50: it is where lifetime delivery cost concentrates (most of a product's life is spent modifying it, not building it the first time). Build carries 0.20 (the feedback loop is paid dozens of times a day), Ship and Run 0.15 each. When an axis has no data it gates out and the headline renormalizes over the measured axes.

scoreGroups.$comment_axes: The 4 delivery axes. Design rules (v2): (1) every scored member is scope:'variant' — repo-level metrics tie across variants by construction, so scoring them only dilutes real differences; they remain in the catalog as displayed context. (2) 2–6 strong members per axis beat 15 fragile ones. (3) No member rewards volume (more tests / more docs / more services / more checks): members measure either a real cost (time, count of things to coordinate) or a real guarantee (coverage of a guard).

build.$comment: Build = the price of one trustworthy signal on a change. Warm re-validation dominates (0.7): a developer re-validates on every change — tens of times a day — while the cold cost is paid on fresh clones and cache-less CI runs (~once a day). Both are REAL measured runs of the variant's own full gates (see variant-runner.mjs). Audited for fairness (final pass): each variant runs ITS OWN declared CI gate over its entire own scope — Flow's nx run-many over 16 projects, Friction's two apps, Overfit's TS chain + cargo (its ci:overfit gate includes clippy/test/release build by its own architectural decision). The warm pass never disables toolchain-native caches: cargo incremental and Next's cache are fully in play for Overfit — its warm≈cold ratio reflects that its stack has NO computation-cache layer (no nx equivalent), which is a real property, not a protocol artifact.
build.label: Build

ship.$comment: Ship v1.1 = the capability to turn a validated change into releasable running software with confidence. It balances delivery confidence (dedicated healthchecked deploys + clean gate output) and delivery coordination/packaging cost (services to coordinate, Docker build time, image size). Docker image size remains a real cost but is deliberately minor; repo-level release/rollback/provenance is shared by all variants and therefore not scored.
ship.label: Ship

run.$comment: Run = the price of understanding production when it misbehaves, and of restoring it. Two families: fragmentation cost (how many runtimes to inspect) and signal QUALITY at those surfaces (dedicated health endpoints, structured leveled logs) — counting only surfaces would make one unreadable log stream beat two well-instrumented services, exactly the naive metric this lab criticizes. Startup = restore speed, from the CI matrix's real container probe. Request-IDs and structured error envelopes were audited and tie across all three variants (all implement them) — a tie adds no comparison, so they are not scored. Lighthouse/browser quality is deliberately NOT here — it measures the product's local excellence, not diagnosability.
run.label: Run

change.$comment: Change = the price of the NEXT modification — the dominant axis, in two families. FOOTPRINT (0.60): where the same product capability lives in each codebase (the risk-trend experiment) — the measured propagation surface. STRUCTURAL (0.40): the codebase properties that EXPLAIN why it spread that way. Double-counting audit: sourceFiles and projects both grow with distribution (breadth vs coordination), combined cap 0.55 — they are the two core reads of propagation; contract restatements overlap the footprint (a duplicated contract file is also a footprint file), so restatements stay at 0.10 (down from 0.25 pre-experiment); testSupport moved to context (non-discriminating today + Rust-idiom classification bias — see $comment_change_footprint).
change.label: Change

nxProjects.label: Workspace projects
nxProjects.description: Buildable projects (npm packages + Rust crates). Scored on balance: a monolith is under-modularized, dozens of projects is over-fragmented; the healthy band is in between.

sourceFiles.label: Source files
sourceFiles.description: Source files (ts/tsx/js/rs/css), excluding tests, config, docs and generated code. Scored on balance.

locTotal.label: Total LOC
locTotal.description: Non-empty lines of source code. Scored on balance — too little means missing structure, too much means bloat.

locBackend.label: Backend LOC
locBackend.description: Lines of back-end / server code (heuristic classification, includes Rust).

todoCount.label: TODO / FIXME
todoCount.description: Occurrences of TODO / FIXME / HACK / XXX markers in source.

anyCount.label: `any` usages
anyCount.description: Uses of the TypeScript `any` escape hatch (`: any`, `as any`, `<any>`).

asUnknownAsCount.label: `as unknown as` casts
asUnknownAsCount.description: Double casts through `unknown` — a strong smell of fighting the type system.

businessRatio.label: Business/boilerplate ratio
businessRatio.description: Share of code that is product logic vs config/generated/barrel boilerplate. Higher = more signal per line.

directDeps.label: Direct dependencies
directDeps.description: Distinct third-party runtime + dev dependencies declared across the variant's manifests.

circularDeps.label: Circular dependencies
circularDeps.description: Import cycles between the variant's own projects (detected from workspace manifests).

graphNodes.label: Graph nodes
graphNodes.description: Projects in the variant's internal dependency graph. Scored on balance.

graphEdges.label: Graph edges
graphEdges.description: Internal dependency edges between the variant's projects.

graphDensity.label: Graph density
graphDensity.description: Edges / possible-edges — how tangled the internal graph is.

fanInAvg.label: Avg fan-in
fanInAvg.description: Average number of projects that depend on a given project.

fanOutAvg.label: Avg fan-out
fanOutAvg.description: Average number of projects a given project depends on.

fanInMax.label: Max fan-in
fanInMax.description: Most-depended-upon project's fan-in — the blast radius of a change to the busiest hub.

fanOutMax.label: Max fan-out
fanOutMax.description: Highest number of dependencies a single project pulls in.

bundleJsKb.label: JS bundle (raw)
bundleJsKb.description: Total uncompressed JavaScript shipped to the browser.

bundleJsGzipKb.label: JS bundle (gzip)
bundleJsGzipKb.description: Gzip-compressed JavaScript — the closest proxy to what users download.

bundleCssKb.label: CSS bundle (raw)
bundleCssKb.description: Total uncompressed CSS shipped to the browser.

largestChunkKb.label: Largest chunk
largestChunkKb.description: Size of the single biggest JS chunk (a hydration-cost proxy).

imagesKb.label: Images
imagesKb.description: Total image weight in the build output.

fontsKb.label: Fonts
fontsKb.description: Total font weight in the build output.

distTotalKb.label: Build artifact
distTotalKb.description: Total size of the deployable build output.

catalog.$comment_variant_ci: Variant-level COLD-VALIDATION metrics (collector: variant-ci, scope: variant). Produced by the measurement matrix in metrics.yml (collect-variant.mjs), which runs each variant's build/typecheck/lint/test with the build cache DISABLED (Flow: nx --skip-nx-cache; Friction/Overfit: pnpm/cargo/next outside the Nx cache). They therefore measure the INTRINSIC, cache-immune cost of the validation protocol and are directly comparable across variants — they are NOT the real cached CI feedback time (see variant.ci.feedback.duration for that). UNLIKE the repo-level GitHub metrics below (which tie across variants), these differentiate Flow / Friction / Overfit. All degrade to `unavailable` when no CI artifact is present (e.g. the default local static run).

variant.ci.build.duration.label: Variant build time (cold)
variant.ci.build.duration.description: Cache-DISABLED cold build of this variant's app(s) (Flow: nx --skip-nx-cache; Friction/Overfit: pnpm/cargo/next). Intrinsic build cost, independent of CI cache strategy. NOT the real cached CI feedback time.

variant.ci.typecheck.duration.label: Variant typecheck time (cold)
variant.ci.typecheck.duration.description: Cache-disabled cold typecheck of this variant. Intrinsic cost, cache-immune.

variant.ci.lint.duration.label: Variant lint time (cold)
variant.ci.lint.duration.description: Cache-disabled cold lint / boundary check of this variant. Intrinsic cost, cache-immune.

variant.ci.test.duration.label: Variant test time (cold)
variant.ci.test.duration.description: Cache-disabled cold unit-test run of this variant (vitest). Intrinsic cost, cache-immune.

variant.ci.validation.cold.label: Full validation (cold)
variant.ci.validation.cold.description: Total cache-disabled cost of the variant's whole validation protocol: build + typecheck + lint + test, summed. The intrinsic worst-case price of one trustworthy signal.

variant.ci.validation.warm.label: Full re-validation (warm)
variant.ci.validation.warm.description: The SAME four gates re-run after the cold pass with the variant's real cache strategy in play, PRIMED first (one untimed cache-enabled run for every variant) so the number is the steady-state daily loop, not a first-run cache miss. The everyday 'is my change still green?' cost — paid dozens of times a day.

variant.ci.tests.executed.label: Tests executed
variant.ci.tests.executed.description: Number of unit tests executed by this variant's suite. Informational (not scored).

variant.ci.tests.passed.label: Tests passed
variant.ci.tests.passed.description: Number of passing unit tests. Informational.

variant.ci.warnings.count.label: CI warnings
variant.ci.warnings.count.description: Warnings parsed from this variant's build/lint/typecheck output (heuristic across tsc/eslint/oxlint/cargo).

variant.ci.errors.count.label: CI gate errors
variant.ci.errors.count.description: Errors parsed from this variant's build/lint/typecheck output (heuristic). Scored in Ship v1.1 as gate cleanliness: the result of the release-control path, not the number of checks.

variant.ci.ramPeak.build.label: Build peak RAM
variant.ci.ramPeak.build.description: Peak resident memory of the build step (from /usr/bin/time on the CI runner). Unavailable where the runner lacks GNU/BSD time.

variant.ci.ramPeak.tests.label: Test peak RAM
variant.ci.ramPeak.tests.description: Peak resident memory of the test step (from /usr/bin/time).

variant.ci.artifact.distSize.label: Variant dist size (CI)
variant.ci.artifact.distSize.description: Build-output size measured in the CI job right after build. Informational — `distTotalKb` (bundle collector) is the scored artifact size.

catalog.$comment_variant_feedback: REAL cached CI feedback (collector: variant-ci, scope: variant, OBSERVATIONAL — axis:null, not scored). Mean delay from a run starting to that variant's PRIMARY quality signal being ready, WITH the variant's real cache strategy (Flow: pnpm+Nx; Overfit: 6-layer + BuildKit; Friction: pnpm store only). Definition B: max(quality_job.completed_at) − run.run_started_at (excludes the GitHub queue, which is runner-availability noise). Population: PUSH-to-main runs only — on main every variant validates its FULL scope, so samples are comparable AND free of the PR `nx affected` no-op (a cross-variant PR that triggers flow-ci with 0 affected flow projects is NOT counted). Conclusions success/failure/timed_out counted; cancelled/skipped excluded. One sample per run (latest attempt). Side jobs (Flow a11y, Overfit warm-cache) excluded via the qualityJobs allow-list. MUST be read next to the scope/size context (nxProjects, sourceFiles, locTotal, variant.ci.tests.executed): same gate categories != same amount of work. Deliberately NOT scored. `unavailable` until a real push-to-main run exists.

variant.ci.feedback.duration.label: CI feedback to signal (main)
variant.ci.feedback.duration.description: Mean delay from a push-to-main run starting to this variant's primary quality signal (its *-ci.yml quality job), with its real cache strategy: max(quality_job.completed_at) − run.run_started_at, over push-to-main runs (conclusions success|failure|timed_out; cancelled/skipped excluded). Observational only (not scored). Interpret with nxProjects / sourceFiles / locTotal — the scopes differ (Friction 2 projects vs Flow 15 vs Overfit 31). `unavailable` until a real push-to-main run exists.

variant.ci.feedback.samples.label: CI feedback samples
variant.ci.feedback.samples.description: Number of push-to-main runs the feedback duration was averaged over. Informational.

variant.docker.build.duration.label: Docker build time
variant.docker.build.duration.description: Wall-clock `docker build --no-cache` for this variant's image. Ship v1.1 keeps it as packaging/pipeline cost, with a reduced weight so it does not double-count Build's feedback-cost axis.

variant.docker.image.size.label: Docker image size
variant.docker.image.size.description: Final image size via `docker image inspect`. Ship v1.1 treats it as artifact transfer/storage/pull context, not a primary confidence signal.

variant.docker.layers.count.label: Docker layers
variant.docker.layers.count.description: RootFS content layers in the image — a proxy for build-cache friendliness.

variant.docker.startup.duration.label: Container startup
variant.docker.startup.duration.description: Time from `docker run` to the container answering its health endpoint. Best-effort.

variant.docker.healthcheck.status.label: Container healthcheck
variant.docker.healthcheck.status.description: Whether the started container served its health endpoint (ok/fail). Informational.

variant.docker.healthcheck.duration.label: Healthcheck latency
variant.docker.healthcheck.duration.description: Latency of the first successful health request once the container is up. Informational.

catalog.$comment_delivery: Deployment/diagnosis surface (collector: delivery, scope: variant). Parsed statically from each variant's REAL deployment artifacts — its Dockerfiles (the same ones release.yml ships to GHCR and docker-compose.prod.yml runs). Deterministic and variant-agnostic: a service is a Dockerfile; more services = more coordination, never more points.

ship.services.count.label: Services to ship
ship.services.count.description: Images the variant must build, push and deploy for one release (its Dockerfiles). Every extra service is an extra build, push, deploy and coordination step.

ship.healthcheck.coverage.label: Guarded deploys
ship.healthcheck.coverage.description: Share of the variant's images whose Docker HEALTHCHECK targets a dedicated health endpoint. This is a deploy-confidence guard, not mere presence of a HEALTHCHECK instruction.

run.inspection.surfaces.label: Runtimes to inspect
run.inspection.surfaces.description: When production misbehaves, how many separate runtimes must be inspected before the fault can even be localized.

run.health.coverage.label: Health endpoint coverage
run.health.coverage.description: Share of the variant's services that expose a dedicated health endpoint (a health route in their source, or a HEALTHCHECK that targets a health path — not just the home page).

run.structuredLogs.present.label: Structured logs
run.structuredLogs.present.description: Does the runtime emit structured, leveled, contextual logs (tracing/tracing_subscriber in Rust, a leveled createLogger in TS) — or only bare console output? Signal QUALITY: one unreadable stream must not beat two well-instrumented services just because it is a single place to look.

catalog.$comment_change_surface: Contract-propagation cost (collector: change-surface, scope: variant). Measured on the shipped-everywhere `riskScore` field of the shared Signal contract: how many hand-written files under the variant's roots RE-DECLARE the field with a type (TS `riskScore: number` / Rust `pub risk_score:`). Importing the shared contract costs 0; every duplicate must be found, edited and kept in sync each time the product's data shape moves.

change.contract.restatements.label: Contract restatements
change.contract.restatements.description: Hand-written files that re-declare the shared Signal contract (measured on the riskScore field). 0 = the variant imports the single source of truth; each extra copy is one more place a schema change must propagate to — and one more place it can silently diverge.

catalog.$comment_change_footprint: Capability FOOTPRINT (collector: change-experiment, scope: variant). The controlled experiment: the SAME product capability (Risk trend, docs/product/03-ai-task-protocol.md) implemented in all three variants against one spec. The collector measures WHERE the capability lives in each codebase today (files carrying its identifiers under the variant's roots) — a deterministic footprint, NOT a historical git diff (Overfit's implementation has no isolable baseline commit; the same footprint method is applied to all three instead of inventing a fake diff). Cross-checked against the real diffs where they exist (Flow ±0, Friction ±2 files). Deliberately REJECTED from scoring: linesChanged (no honest baseline for Overfit; rewards terseness over readability), boundariesCrossed (no variant-agnostic taxonomy survives scrutiny — projects is the honest proxy), validationCost (already Build's warm/cold gates — double count), testSupport (5/5/5 today — non-discriminating — and an exclusive source-XOR-test split biases against Rust's idiomatic inline #[cfg(test)] modules; kept as NON-exclusive context).

change.footprint.sourceFiles.label: Footprint — source files
change.footprint.sourceFiles.description: Hand-written production source files that carry the Risk trend capability — the propagation surface of one identical product intent in this codebase. A footprint (where the capability lives today), not a git diff.

change.footprint.testSupport.label: Footprint — test support
change.footprint.testSupport.description: Test surface behind the capability: dedicated test files PLUS Rust sources with inline #[cfg(test)] modules (non-exclusive with source files). Context only — not scored: it ties across variants today and an exclusive classification would bias against Rust idioms.

change.footprint.docs.label: Footprint — documentation
change.footprint.docs.description: Hand-maintained documentation files that carry the capability (ADRs, policies, governance). A synchronization surface: every copy must stay coherent when the capability moves. Not a judgment that documentation is bad — a measure of what must be kept in sync.

change.footprint.projects.label: Footprint — projects
change.footprint.projects.description: Distinct workspace projects (packages/crates/apps) the capability's footprint spans — the coordination surface of one change.

change.footprint.files.label: Footprint — total files
change.footprint.files.description: Total hand-written files carrying the capability (source + dedicated tests + docs + config). Context — the scored members are the classified counts.

change.footprint.generated.label: Footprint — generated files
change.footprint.generated.description: Generated mirrors (OpenAPI spec, generated clients) that carry the capability. Context — regenerating is cheap; the hand-written counts are what score.

requests.label: Initial requests
requests.description: Number of network requests to load the main route.

transferredKb.label: Transferred weight
transferredKb.description: Total bytes transferred over the wire for the main route.

co2PerViewMg.label: CO₂ / page view
co2PerViewMg.description: Estimated CO₂ per page view (SWD model from transferred bytes).

catalog.$comment_github: Repo-level delivery-pipeline metrics from the GitHub Actions API (collector: github, scope: repo). The three variants SHARE one monorepo CI pipeline, so these numbers describe the shared delivery chain — they are identical for all variants and therefore tie at 100 during min-max normalization. They enrich the Ship/Change axes with the real cost of shipping without ever faking per-variant differentiation. All degrade to `unavailable` (score falls back to the differentiating members) when no GITHUB token is present.

ship.ci.wallTime.avg.label: CI wall time (avg)
ship.ci.wallTime.avg.description: Mean wall-clock time of the last N completed workflow runs (updated_at − run_started_at). Repo-level.

ship.ci.wallTime.p95.label: CI wall time (p95)
ship.ci.wallTime.p95.description: 95th-percentile wall-clock run time — the tail a developer occasionally waits through. Repo-level (display only).

ship.ci.successRate.lastN.label: CI success rate
ship.ci.successRate.lastN.description: Successful ÷ completed runs over the last N. Repo-level.

ship.ci.flakyProxy.rate.label: CI instability proxy
ship.ci.flakyProxy.rate.description: Share of jobs that both passed AND failed across the sampled runs. A job-level instability proxy — NOT a test-level flaky rate. Repo-level.

ship.ci.artifacts.totalSize.label: CI artifacts (total)
ship.ci.artifacts.totalSize.description: Total size of non-expired workflow artifacts. Repo-level.

ship.deploy.avgDuration.label: Deploy duration (avg)
ship.deploy.avgDuration.description: Average deployment duration (GitHub Deployments API, else deploy-named Actions jobs). Repo-level; unavailable if neither exists.

change.pr.avgChangedFiles.label: PR changed files (avg)
change.pr.avgChangedFiles.description: Average files touched per merged PR — a proxy for change surface. Repo-level, mixes all chantiers; not a controlled per-variant scenario.

change.pr.avgAdditions.label: PR additions (avg)
change.pr.avgAdditions.description: Average lines added per merged PR. Repo-level (display only).

change.pr.avgDeletions.label: PR deletions (avg)
change.pr.avgDeletions.description: Average lines deleted per merged PR. Repo-level (display only).

change.pr.avgTimeToMerge.label: PR time to merge (avg)
change.pr.avgTimeToMerge.description: Average created→merged duration for merged PRs — a proxy for review/merge latency. Repo-level.

change.pr.avgReviewCount.label: PR reviews (avg)
change.pr.avgReviewCount.description: Average number of reviews per merged PR. Repo-level (display only).

meta.label: Flow
meta.thesis: The balanced choice — modular, readable, and the cheapest to change.

nxProjects.label: Workspace projects
nxProjects.description: Buildable projects (npm packages + Rust crates). Scored on balance: a monolith is under-modularized, dozens of projects is over-fragmented; the healthy band is in between.

sourceFiles.label: Source files
sourceFiles.description: Source files (ts/tsx/js/rs/css), excluding tests, config, docs and generated code. Scored on balance.

locTotal.label: Total LOC
locTotal.description: Non-empty lines of source code. Scored on balance — too little means missing structure, too much means bloat.

locBackend.label: Backend LOC
locBackend.description: Lines of back-end / server code (heuristic classification, includes Rust).

todoCount.label: TODO / FIXME
todoCount.description: Occurrences of TODO / FIXME / HACK / XXX markers in source.

anyCount.label: `any` usages
anyCount.description: Uses of the TypeScript `any` escape hatch (`: any`, `as any`, `<any>`).

asUnknownAsCount.label: `as unknown as` casts
asUnknownAsCount.description: Double casts through `unknown` — a strong smell of fighting the type system.

businessRatio.label: Business/boilerplate ratio
businessRatio.description: Share of code that is product logic vs config/generated/barrel boilerplate. Higher = more signal per line.

directDeps.label: Direct dependencies
directDeps.description: Distinct third-party runtime + dev dependencies declared across the variant's manifests.

circularDeps.label: Circular dependencies
circularDeps.description: Import cycles between the variant's own projects (detected from workspace manifests).

graphNodes.label: Graph nodes
graphNodes.description: Projects in the variant's internal dependency graph. Scored on balance.

graphEdges.label: Graph edges
graphEdges.description: Internal dependency edges between the variant's projects.

graphDensity.label: Graph density
graphDensity.description: Edges / possible-edges — how tangled the internal graph is.

fanInAvg.label: Avg fan-in
fanInAvg.description: Average number of projects that depend on a given project.

fanOutAvg.label: Avg fan-out
fanOutAvg.description: Average number of projects a given project depends on.

fanInMax.label: Max fan-in
fanInMax.description: Most-depended-upon project's fan-in — the blast radius of a change to the busiest hub.

fanOutMax.label: Max fan-out
fanOutMax.description: Highest number of dependencies a single project pulls in.

bundleJsKb.label: JS bundle (raw)
bundleJsKb.description: Total uncompressed JavaScript shipped to the browser.

bundleJsGzipKb.label: JS bundle (gzip)
bundleJsGzipKb.description: Gzip-compressed JavaScript — the closest proxy to what users download.

bundleCssKb.label: CSS bundle (raw)
bundleCssKb.description: Total uncompressed CSS shipped to the browser.

largestChunkKb.label: Largest chunk
largestChunkKb.description: Size of the single biggest JS chunk (a hydration-cost proxy).

imagesKb.label: Images
imagesKb.description: Total image weight in the build output.

fontsKb.label: Fonts
fontsKb.description: Total font weight in the build output.

distTotalKb.label: Build artifact
distTotalKb.description: Total size of the deployable build output.

variant.ci.build.duration.label: Variant build time (cold)
variant.ci.build.duration.description: Cache-DISABLED cold build of this variant's app(s) (Flow: nx --skip-nx-cache; Friction/Overfit: pnpm/cargo/next). Intrinsic build cost, independent of CI cache strategy. NOT the real cached CI feedback time.

variant.ci.typecheck.duration.label: Variant typecheck time (cold)
variant.ci.typecheck.duration.description: Cache-disabled cold typecheck of this variant. Intrinsic cost, cache-immune.

variant.ci.lint.duration.label: Variant lint time (cold)
variant.ci.lint.duration.description: Cache-disabled cold lint / boundary check of this variant. Intrinsic cost, cache-immune.

variant.ci.test.duration.label: Variant test time (cold)
variant.ci.test.duration.description: Cache-disabled cold unit-test run of this variant (vitest). Intrinsic cost, cache-immune.

variant.ci.validation.cold.label: Full validation (cold)
variant.ci.validation.cold.description: Total cache-disabled cost of the variant's whole validation protocol: build + typecheck + lint + test, summed. The intrinsic worst-case price of one trustworthy signal.

variant.ci.validation.warm.label: Full re-validation (warm)
variant.ci.validation.warm.description: The SAME four gates re-run after the cold pass with the variant's real cache strategy in play, PRIMED first (one untimed cache-enabled run for every variant) so the number is the steady-state daily loop, not a first-run cache miss. The everyday 'is my change still green?' cost — paid dozens of times a day.

variant.ci.tests.executed.label: Tests executed
variant.ci.tests.executed.description: Number of unit tests executed by this variant's suite. Informational (not scored).

variant.ci.tests.passed.label: Tests passed
variant.ci.tests.passed.description: Number of passing unit tests. Informational.

variant.ci.warnings.count.label: CI warnings
variant.ci.warnings.count.description: Warnings parsed from this variant's build/lint/typecheck output (heuristic across tsc/eslint/oxlint/cargo).

variant.ci.errors.count.label: CI gate errors
variant.ci.errors.count.description: Errors parsed from this variant's build/lint/typecheck output (heuristic). Scored in Ship v1.1 as gate cleanliness: the result of the release-control path, not the number of checks.

variant.ci.ramPeak.build.label: Build peak RAM
variant.ci.ramPeak.build.description: Peak resident memory of the build step (from /usr/bin/time on the CI runner). Unavailable where the runner lacks GNU/BSD time.

variant.ci.ramPeak.tests.label: Test peak RAM
variant.ci.ramPeak.tests.description: Peak resident memory of the test step (from /usr/bin/time).

variant.ci.artifact.distSize.label: Variant dist size (CI)
variant.ci.artifact.distSize.description: Build-output size measured in the CI job right after build. Informational — `distTotalKb` (bundle collector) is the scored artifact size.

variant.ci.feedback.duration.label: CI feedback to signal (main)
variant.ci.feedback.duration.description: Mean delay from a push-to-main run starting to this variant's primary quality signal (its *-ci.yml quality job), with its real cache strategy: max(quality_job.completed_at) − run.run_started_at, over push-to-main runs (conclusions success|failure|timed_out; cancelled/skipped excluded). Observational only (not scored). Interpret with nxProjects / sourceFiles / locTotal — the scopes differ (Friction 2 projects vs Flow 15 vs Overfit 31). `unavailable` until a real push-to-main run exists.

variant.ci.feedback.samples.label: CI feedback samples
variant.ci.feedback.samples.description: Number of push-to-main runs the feedback duration was averaged over. Informational.

variant.docker.build.duration.label: Docker build time
variant.docker.build.duration.description: Wall-clock `docker build --no-cache` for this variant's image. Ship v1.1 keeps it as packaging/pipeline cost, with a reduced weight so it does not double-count Build's feedback-cost axis.

variant.docker.image.size.label: Docker image size
variant.docker.image.size.description: Final image size via `docker image inspect`. Ship v1.1 treats it as artifact transfer/storage/pull context, not a primary confidence signal.

variant.docker.layers.count.label: Docker layers
variant.docker.layers.count.description: RootFS content layers in the image — a proxy for build-cache friendliness.

variant.docker.startup.duration.label: Container startup
variant.docker.startup.duration.description: Time from `docker run` to the container answering its health endpoint. Best-effort.

variant.docker.healthcheck.status.label: Container healthcheck
variant.docker.healthcheck.status.description: Whether the started container served its health endpoint (ok/fail). Informational.

variant.docker.healthcheck.duration.label: Healthcheck latency
variant.docker.healthcheck.duration.description: Latency of the first successful health request once the container is up. Informational.

ship.services.count.label: Services to ship
ship.services.count.description: Images the variant must build, push and deploy for one release (its Dockerfiles). Every extra service is an extra build, push, deploy and coordination step.

ship.healthcheck.coverage.label: Guarded deploys
ship.healthcheck.coverage.description: Share of the variant's images whose Docker HEALTHCHECK targets a dedicated health endpoint. This is a deploy-confidence guard, not mere presence of a HEALTHCHECK instruction.

run.inspection.surfaces.label: Runtimes to inspect
run.inspection.surfaces.description: When production misbehaves, how many separate runtimes must be inspected before the fault can even be localized.

run.health.coverage.label: Health endpoint coverage
run.health.coverage.description: Share of the variant's services that expose a dedicated health endpoint (a health route in their source, or a HEALTHCHECK that targets a health path — not just the home page).

run.structuredLogs.present.method: leveled/contextual logger invoked by non-test runtime code (tracing/tracing_subscriber, createLogger, logger.info|warn|error)
run.structuredLogs.present.label: Structured logs
run.structuredLogs.present.description: Does the runtime emit structured, leveled, contextual logs (tracing/tracing_subscriber in Rust, a leveled createLogger in TS) — or only bare console output? Signal QUALITY: one unreadable stream must not beat two well-instrumented services just because it is a single place to look.

change.contract.restatements.method: files under the variant roots that DECLARE the field (TS `riskScore: number` / Rust `pub risk_score:`), hand-written code only
change.contract.restatements.label: Contract restatements
change.contract.restatements.description: Hand-written files that re-declare the shared Signal contract (measured on the riskScore field). 0 = the variant imports the single source of truth; each extra copy is one more place a schema change must propagate to — and one more place it can silently diverge.

change.footprint.sourceFiles.label: Footprint — source files
change.footprint.sourceFiles.description: Hand-written production source files that carry the Risk trend capability — the propagation surface of one identical product intent in this codebase. A footprint (where the capability lives today), not a git diff.

change.footprint.testSupport.note: NON-exclusive: dedicated test files + source files with inline #[cfg(test)] modules. Context only — overlaps sourceFiles by design.
change.footprint.testSupport.label: Footprint — test support
change.footprint.testSupport.description: Test surface behind the capability: dedicated test files PLUS Rust sources with inline #[cfg(test)] modules (non-exclusive with source files). Context only — not scored: it ties across variants today and an exclusive classification would bias against Rust idioms.

change.footprint.docs.label: Footprint — documentation
change.footprint.docs.description: Hand-maintained documentation files that carry the capability (ADRs, policies, governance). A synchronization surface: every copy must stay coherent when the capability moves. Not a judgment that documentation is bad — a measure of what must be kept in sync.

change.footprint.projects.label: Footprint — projects
change.footprint.projects.description: Distinct workspace projects (packages/crates/apps) the capability's footprint spans — the coordination surface of one change.

change.footprint.files.note: hand-written files carrying the capability (source + dedicated tests + docs + config); generated mirrors counted separately
change.footprint.files.label: Footprint — total files
change.footprint.files.description: Total hand-written files carrying the capability (source + dedicated tests + docs + config). Context — the scored members are the classified counts.

change.footprint.generated.label: Footprint — generated files
change.footprint.generated.description: Generated mirrors (OpenAPI spec, generated clients) that carry the capability. Context — regenerating is cheap; the hand-written counts are what score.

transferredKb.label: Transferred weight
transferredKb.description: Total bytes transferred over the wire for the main route.

co2PerViewMg.reason: No .lighthouseci reports present — run the Lighthouse audit.
co2PerViewMg.label: CO₂ / page view
co2PerViewMg.description: Estimated CO₂ per page view (SWD model from transferred bytes).

ship.ci.wallTime.avg.label: CI wall time (avg)
ship.ci.wallTime.avg.description: Mean wall-clock time of the last N completed workflow runs (updated_at − run_started_at). Repo-level.

ship.ci.wallTime.p95.label: CI wall time (p95)
ship.ci.wallTime.p95.description: 95th-percentile wall-clock run time — the tail a developer occasionally waits through. Repo-level (display only).

ship.ci.successRate.lastN.label: CI success rate
ship.ci.successRate.lastN.description: Successful ÷ completed runs over the last N. Repo-level.

ship.ci.flakyProxy.rate.label: CI instability proxy
ship.ci.flakyProxy.rate.description: Share of jobs that both passed AND failed across the sampled runs. A job-level instability proxy — NOT a test-level flaky rate. Repo-level.

ship.ci.artifacts.totalSize.label: CI artifacts (total)
ship.ci.artifacts.totalSize.description: Total size of non-expired workflow artifacts. Repo-level.

ship.deploy.avgDuration.label: Deploy duration (avg)
ship.deploy.avgDuration.description: Average deployment duration (GitHub Deployments API, else deploy-named Actions jobs). Repo-level; unavailable if neither exists.

change.pr.avgChangedFiles.label: PR changed files (avg)
change.pr.avgChangedFiles.description: Average files touched per merged PR — a proxy for change surface. Repo-level, mixes all chantiers; not a controlled per-variant scenario.

change.pr.avgAdditions.label: PR additions (avg)
change.pr.avgAdditions.description: Average lines added per merged PR. Repo-level (display only).

change.pr.avgDeletions.label: PR deletions (avg)
change.pr.avgDeletions.description: Average lines deleted per merged PR. Repo-level (display only).

change.pr.avgTimeToMerge.label: PR time to merge (avg)
change.pr.avgTimeToMerge.description: Average created→merged duration for merged PRs — a proxy for review/merge latency. Repo-level.

change.pr.avgReviewCount.label: PR reviews (avg)
change.pr.avgReviewCount.description: Average number of reviews per merged PR. Repo-level (display only).

meta.label: Friction
meta.thesis: Locally fast, globally expensive — fewer parts, tangled and harder to trust.

nxProjects.label: Workspace projects
nxProjects.description: Buildable projects (npm packages + Rust crates). Scored on balance: a monolith is under-modularized, dozens of projects is over-fragmented; the healthy band is in between.

sourceFiles.label: Source files
sourceFiles.description: Source files (ts/tsx/js/rs/css), excluding tests, config, docs and generated code. Scored on balance.

locTotal.label: Total LOC
locTotal.description: Non-empty lines of source code. Scored on balance — too little means missing structure, too much means bloat.

locBackend.label: Backend LOC
locBackend.description: Lines of back-end / server code (heuristic classification, includes Rust).

todoCount.label: TODO / FIXME
todoCount.description: Occurrences of TODO / FIXME / HACK / XXX markers in source.

anyCount.label: `any` usages
anyCount.description: Uses of the TypeScript `any` escape hatch (`: any`, `as any`, `<any>`).

asUnknownAsCount.label: `as unknown as` casts
asUnknownAsCount.description: Double casts through `unknown` — a strong smell of fighting the type system.

businessRatio.label: Business/boilerplate ratio
businessRatio.description: Share of code that is product logic vs config/generated/barrel boilerplate. Higher = more signal per line.

directDeps.label: Direct dependencies
directDeps.description: Distinct third-party runtime + dev dependencies declared across the variant's manifests.

circularDeps.label: Circular dependencies
circularDeps.description: Import cycles between the variant's own projects (detected from workspace manifests).

graphNodes.label: Graph nodes
graphNodes.description: Projects in the variant's internal dependency graph. Scored on balance.

graphEdges.label: Graph edges
graphEdges.description: Internal dependency edges between the variant's projects.

graphDensity.label: Graph density
graphDensity.description: Edges / possible-edges — how tangled the internal graph is.

fanInAvg.label: Avg fan-in
fanInAvg.description: Average number of projects that depend on a given project.

fanOutAvg.label: Avg fan-out
fanOutAvg.description: Average number of projects a given project depends on.

fanInMax.label: Max fan-in
fanInMax.description: Most-depended-upon project's fan-in — the blast radius of a change to the busiest hub.

fanOutMax.label: Max fan-out
fanOutMax.description: Highest number of dependencies a single project pulls in.

bundleJsKb.label: JS bundle (raw)
bundleJsKb.description: Total uncompressed JavaScript shipped to the browser.

bundleJsGzipKb.label: JS bundle (gzip)
bundleJsGzipKb.description: Gzip-compressed JavaScript — the closest proxy to what users download.

bundleCssKb.label: CSS bundle (raw)
bundleCssKb.description: Total uncompressed CSS shipped to the browser.

largestChunkKb.label: Largest chunk
largestChunkKb.description: Size of the single biggest JS chunk (a hydration-cost proxy).

imagesKb.label: Images
imagesKb.description: Total image weight in the build output.

fontsKb.label: Fonts
fontsKb.description: Total font weight in the build output.

distTotalKb.label: Build artifact
distTotalKb.description: Total size of the deployable build output.

variant.ci.build.duration.label: Variant build time (cold)
variant.ci.build.duration.description: Cache-DISABLED cold build of this variant's app(s) (Flow: nx --skip-nx-cache; Friction/Overfit: pnpm/cargo/next). Intrinsic build cost, independent of CI cache strategy. NOT the real cached CI feedback time.

variant.ci.typecheck.duration.label: Variant typecheck time (cold)
variant.ci.typecheck.duration.description: Cache-disabled cold typecheck of this variant. Intrinsic cost, cache-immune.

variant.ci.lint.duration.label: Variant lint time (cold)
variant.ci.lint.duration.description: Cache-disabled cold lint / boundary check of this variant. Intrinsic cost, cache-immune.

variant.ci.test.duration.label: Variant test time (cold)
variant.ci.test.duration.description: Cache-disabled cold unit-test run of this variant (vitest). Intrinsic cost, cache-immune.

variant.ci.validation.cold.label: Full validation (cold)
variant.ci.validation.cold.description: Total cache-disabled cost of the variant's whole validation protocol: build + typecheck + lint + test, summed. The intrinsic worst-case price of one trustworthy signal.

variant.ci.validation.warm.label: Full re-validation (warm)
variant.ci.validation.warm.description: The SAME four gates re-run after the cold pass with the variant's real cache strategy in play, PRIMED first (one untimed cache-enabled run for every variant) so the number is the steady-state daily loop, not a first-run cache miss. The everyday 'is my change still green?' cost — paid dozens of times a day.

variant.ci.tests.executed.label: Tests executed
variant.ci.tests.executed.description: Number of unit tests executed by this variant's suite. Informational (not scored).

variant.ci.tests.passed.label: Tests passed
variant.ci.tests.passed.description: Number of passing unit tests. Informational.

variant.ci.warnings.count.label: CI warnings
variant.ci.warnings.count.description: Warnings parsed from this variant's build/lint/typecheck output (heuristic across tsc/eslint/oxlint/cargo).

variant.ci.errors.count.label: CI gate errors
variant.ci.errors.count.description: Errors parsed from this variant's build/lint/typecheck output (heuristic). Scored in Ship v1.1 as gate cleanliness: the result of the release-control path, not the number of checks.

variant.ci.ramPeak.build.label: Build peak RAM
variant.ci.ramPeak.build.description: Peak resident memory of the build step (from /usr/bin/time on the CI runner). Unavailable where the runner lacks GNU/BSD time.

variant.ci.ramPeak.tests.label: Test peak RAM
variant.ci.ramPeak.tests.description: Peak resident memory of the test step (from /usr/bin/time).

variant.ci.artifact.distSize.label: Variant dist size (CI)
variant.ci.artifact.distSize.description: Build-output size measured in the CI job right after build. Informational — `distTotalKb` (bundle collector) is the scored artifact size.

variant.ci.feedback.duration.label: CI feedback to signal (main)
variant.ci.feedback.duration.description: Mean delay from a push-to-main run starting to this variant's primary quality signal (its *-ci.yml quality job), with its real cache strategy: max(quality_job.completed_at) − run.run_started_at, over push-to-main runs (conclusions success|failure|timed_out; cancelled/skipped excluded). Observational only (not scored). Interpret with nxProjects / sourceFiles / locTotal — the scopes differ (Friction 2 projects vs Flow 15 vs Overfit 31). `unavailable` until a real push-to-main run exists.

variant.ci.feedback.samples.label: CI feedback samples
variant.ci.feedback.samples.description: Number of push-to-main runs the feedback duration was averaged over. Informational.

variant.docker.build.duration.label: Docker build time
variant.docker.build.duration.description: Wall-clock `docker build --no-cache` for this variant's image. Ship v1.1 keeps it as packaging/pipeline cost, with a reduced weight so it does not double-count Build's feedback-cost axis.

variant.docker.image.size.label: Docker image size
variant.docker.image.size.description: Final image size via `docker image inspect`. Ship v1.1 treats it as artifact transfer/storage/pull context, not a primary confidence signal.

variant.docker.layers.count.label: Docker layers
variant.docker.layers.count.description: RootFS content layers in the image — a proxy for build-cache friendliness.

variant.docker.startup.duration.label: Container startup
variant.docker.startup.duration.description: Time from `docker run` to the container answering its health endpoint. Best-effort.

variant.docker.healthcheck.status.label: Container healthcheck
variant.docker.healthcheck.status.description: Whether the started container served its health endpoint (ok/fail). Informational.

variant.docker.healthcheck.duration.label: Healthcheck latency
variant.docker.healthcheck.duration.description: Latency of the first successful health request once the container is up. Informational.

ship.services.count.label: Services to ship
ship.services.count.description: Images the variant must build, push and deploy for one release (its Dockerfiles). Every extra service is an extra build, push, deploy and coordination step.

ship.healthcheck.coverage.label: Guarded deploys
ship.healthcheck.coverage.description: Share of the variant's images whose Docker HEALTHCHECK targets a dedicated health endpoint. This is a deploy-confidence guard, not mere presence of a HEALTHCHECK instruction.

run.inspection.surfaces.label: Runtimes to inspect
run.inspection.surfaces.description: When production misbehaves, how many separate runtimes must be inspected before the fault can even be localized.

run.health.coverage.label: Health endpoint coverage
run.health.coverage.description: Share of the variant's services that expose a dedicated health endpoint (a health route in their source, or a HEALTHCHECK that targets a health path — not just the home page).

run.structuredLogs.present.method: leveled/contextual logger invoked by non-test runtime code (tracing/tracing_subscriber, createLogger, logger.info|warn|error)
run.structuredLogs.present.label: Structured logs
run.structuredLogs.present.description: Does the runtime emit structured, leveled, contextual logs (tracing/tracing_subscriber in Rust, a leveled createLogger in TS) — or only bare console output? Signal QUALITY: one unreadable stream must not beat two well-instrumented services just because it is a single place to look.

change.contract.restatements.method: files under the variant roots that DECLARE the field (TS `riskScore: number` / Rust `pub risk_score:`), hand-written code only
change.contract.restatements.label: Contract restatements
change.contract.restatements.description: Hand-written files that re-declare the shared Signal contract (measured on the riskScore field). 0 = the variant imports the single source of truth; each extra copy is one more place a schema change must propagate to — and one more place it can silently diverge.

change.footprint.sourceFiles.description: Hand-written production source files that carry the Risk trend capability — the propagation surface of one identical product intent in this codebase. A footprint (where the capability lives today), not a git diff.

change.footprint.testSupport.note: NON-exclusive: dedicated test files + source files with inline #[cfg(test)] modules. Context only — overlaps sourceFiles by design.
change.footprint.testSupport.label: Footprint — test support
change.footprint.testSupport.description: Test surface behind the capability: dedicated test files PLUS Rust sources with inline #[cfg(test)] modules (non-exclusive with source files). Context only — not scored: it ties across variants today and an exclusive classification would bias against Rust idioms.

change.footprint.docs.label: Footprint — documentation
change.footprint.docs.description: Hand-maintained documentation files that carry the capability (ADRs, policies, governance). A synchronization surface: every copy must stay coherent when the capability moves. Not a judgment that documentation is bad — a measure of what must be kept in sync.

change.footprint.projects.label: Footprint — projects
change.footprint.projects.description: Distinct workspace projects (packages/crates/apps) the capability's footprint spans — the coordination surface of one change.

change.footprint.files.note: hand-written files carrying the capability (source + dedicated tests + docs + config); generated mirrors counted separately
change.footprint.files.label: Footprint — total files
change.footprint.files.description: Total hand-written files carrying the capability (source + dedicated tests + docs + config). Context — the scored members are the classified counts.

change.footprint.generated.label: Footprint — generated files
change.footprint.generated.description: Generated mirrors (OpenAPI spec, generated clients) that carry the capability. Context — regenerating is cheap; the hand-written counts are what score.

variant.docker.build.duration.description: Wall-clock `docker build --no-cache` for this variant's image. Ship v1.1 keeps it as packaging/pipeline cost, with a reduced weight so it does not double-count Build's feedback-cost axis.

variant.docker.image.size.label: Docker image size
variant.docker.image.size.description: Final image size via `docker image inspect`. Ship v1.1 treats it as artifact transfer/storage/pull context, not a primary confidence signal.

variant.docker.layers.count.label: Docker layers
variant.docker.layers.count.description: RootFS content layers in the image — a proxy for build-cache friendliness.

variant.docker.startup.duration.label: Container startup
variant.docker.startup.duration.description: Time from `docker run` to the container answering its health endpoint. Best-effort.

variant.docker.healthcheck.status.label: Container healthcheck
variant.docker.healthcheck.status.description: Whether the started container served its health endpoint (ok/fail). Informational.

variant.docker.healthcheck.duration.label: Healthcheck latency
variant.docker.healthcheck.duration.description: Latency of the first successful health request once the container is up. Informational.

ship.services.count.label: Services to ship
ship.services.count.description: Images the variant must build, push and deploy for one release (its Dockerfiles). Every extra service is an extra build, push, deploy and coordination step.

ship.healthcheck.coverage.label: Guarded deploys
ship.healthcheck.coverage.description: Share of the variant's images whose Docker HEALTHCHECK targets a dedicated health endpoint. This is a deploy-confidence guard, not mere presence of a HEALTHCHECK instruction.

run.inspection.surfaces.label: Runtimes to inspect
run.inspection.surfaces.description: When production misbehaves, how many separate runtimes must be inspected before the fault can even be localized.

run.health.coverage.label: Health endpoint coverage
run.health.coverage.description: Share of the variant's services that expose a dedicated health endpoint (a health route in their source, or a HEALTHCHECK that targets a health path — not just the home page).

run.structuredLogs.present.method: leveled/contextual logger invoked by non-test runtime code (tracing/tracing_subscriber, createLogger, logger.info|warn|error)
run.structuredLogs.present.label: Structured logs
run.structuredLogs.present.description: Does the runtime emit structured, leveled, contextual logs (tracing/tracing_subscriber in Rust, a leveled createLogger in TS) — or only bare console output? Signal QUALITY: one unreadable stream must not beat two well-instrumented services just because it is a single place to look.

change.contract.restatements.method: files under the variant roots that DECLARE the field (TS `riskScore: number` / Rust `pub risk_score:`), hand-written code only
change.contract.restatements.label: Contract restatements
change.contract.restatements.description: Hand-written files that re-declare the shared Signal contract (measured on the riskScore field). 0 = the variant imports the single source of truth; each extra copy is one more place a schema change must propagate to — and one more place it can silently diverge.

change.footprint.sourceFiles.label: Footprint — source files
change.footprint.sourceFiles.description: Hand-written production source files that carry the Risk trend capability — the propagation surface of one identical product intent in this codebase. A footprint (where the capability lives today), not a git diff.

change.footprint.testSupport.note: NON-exclusive: dedicated test files + source files with inline #[cfg(test)] modules. Context only — overlaps sourceFiles by design.
change.footprint.testSupport.label: Footprint — test support
change.footprint.testSupport.description: Test surface behind the capability: dedicated test files PLUS Rust sources with inline #[cfg(test)] modules (non-exclusive with source files). Context only — not scored: it ties across variants today and an exclusive classification would bias against Rust idioms.

change.footprint.docs.label: Footprint — documentation
change.footprint.docs.description: Hand-maintained documentation files that carry the capability (ADRs, policies, governance). A synchronization surface: every copy must stay coherent when the capability moves. Not a judgment that documentation is bad — a measure of what must be kept in sync.

change.footprint.projects.label: Footprint — projects
change.footprint.projects.description: Distinct workspace projects (packages/crates/apps) the capability's footprint spans — the coordination surface of one change.

change.footprint.files.note: hand-written files carrying the capability (source + dedicated tests + docs + config); generated mirrors counted separately
change.footprint.files.label: Footprint — total files
change.footprint.files.description: Total hand-written files carrying the capability (source + dedicated tests + docs + config). Context — the scored members are the classified counts.

change.footprint.generated.label: Footprint — generated files
change.footprint.generated.description: Generated mirrors (OpenAPI spec, generated clients) that carry the capability. Context — regenerating is cheap; the hand-written counts are what score.

lighthousePerformance.reason: No .lighthouseci reports present — run the Lighthouse audit.
lighthousePerformance.label: Lighthouse Performance

lighthouseAccessibility.reason: No .lighthouseci reports present — run the Lighthouse audit.
lighthouseAccessibility.label: Lighthouse Accessibility

lighthouseBestPractices.reason: No .lighthouseci reports present — run the Lighthouse audit.
lighthouseBestPractices.label: Lighthouse Best Practices

lcpMs.reason: No .lighthouseci reports present — run the Lighthouse audit.
lcpMs.label: LCP

cls.reason: No .lighthouseci reports present — run the Lighthouse audit.
cls.label: CLS

tbtMs.reason: No .lighthouseci reports present — run the Lighthouse audit.
tbtMs.label: TBT

speedIndexMs.reason: No .lighthouseci reports present — run the Lighthouse audit.
speedIndexMs.label: Speed Index

fcpMs.reason: No .lighthouseci reports present — run the Lighthouse audit.
fcpMs.label: FCP

requests.reason: No .lighthouseci reports present — run the Lighthouse audit.
requests.label: Initial requests
requests.description: Number of network requests to load the main route.

transferredKb.reason: No .lighthouseci reports present — run the Lighthouse audit.
transferredKb.label: Transferred weight
transferredKb.description: Total bytes transferred over the wire for the main route.

co2PerViewMg.reason: No .lighthouseci reports present — run the Lighthouse audit.
co2PerViewMg.label: CO₂ / page view
co2PerViewMg.description: Estimated CO₂ per page view (SWD model from transferred bytes).

ship.ci.wallTime.avg.label: CI wall time (avg)
ship.ci.wallTime.avg.description: Mean wall-clock time of the last N completed workflow runs (updated_at − run_started_at). Repo-level.

ship.ci.wallTime.p95.label: CI wall time (p95)
ship.ci.wallTime.p95.description: 95th-percentile wall-clock run time — the tail a developer occasionally waits through. Repo-level (display only).

ship.ci.successRate.lastN.label: CI success rate
ship.ci.successRate.lastN.description: Successful ÷ completed runs over the last N. Repo-level.

ship.ci.flakyProxy.rate.label: CI instability proxy
ship.ci.flakyProxy.rate.description: Share of jobs that both passed AND failed across the sampled runs. A job-level instability proxy — NOT a test-level flaky rate. Repo-level.

ship.ci.artifacts.totalSize.label: CI artifacts (total)
ship.ci.artifacts.totalSize.description: Total size of non-expired workflow artifacts. Repo-level.

ship.deploy.avgDuration.label: Deploy duration (avg)
ship.deploy.avgDuration.description: Average deployment duration (GitHub Deployments API, else deploy-named Actions jobs). Repo-level; unavailable if neither exists.

change.pr.avgChangedFiles.label: PR changed files (avg)
change.pr.avgChangedFiles.description: Average files touched per merged PR — a proxy for change surface. Repo-level, mixes all chantiers; not a controlled per-variant scenario.

change.pr.avgAdditions.label: PR additions (avg)
change.pr.avgAdditions.description: Average lines added per merged PR. Repo-level (display only).

change.pr.avgDeletions.label: PR deletions (avg)
change.pr.avgDeletions.description: Average lines deleted per merged PR. Repo-level (display only).

change.pr.avgTimeToMerge.label: PR time to merge (avg)
change.pr.avgTimeToMerge.description: Average created→merged duration for merged PRs — a proxy for review/merge latency. Repo-level.

change.pr.avgReviewCount.label: PR reviews (avg)
change.pr.avgReviewCount.description: Average number of reviews per merged PR. Repo-level (display only).

runtime.reason: No deployed URLs configured; live probing skipped.
runtime.seam: tools/metrics/collectors/runtime.mjs — probe appUrl/apiUrl for healthcheck + latency percentiles.

frontendErrors.reason: Playwright console/error capture not run in this pass.
frontendErrors.seam: tools/metrics/collectors/playwright.mjs — drive the app, capture console errors/warnings, network errors, table/filter latency.

axe.reason: axe-core run not executed in this pass (Lighthouse a11y score is available).
axe.seam: tools/metrics/collectors/axe.mjs — run @axe-core/playwright, bucket violations by impact.

ship.ci.wallTime.avg.label: CI wall time (avg)
ship.ci.wallTime.avg.description: Mean wall-clock time of the last N completed workflow runs (updated_at − run_started_at). Repo-level.

ship.ci.wallTime.p95.label: CI wall time (p95)
ship.ci.wallTime.p95.description: 95th-percentile wall-clock run time — the tail a developer occasionally waits through. Repo-level (display only).

ship.ci.successRate.lastN.label: CI success rate
ship.ci.successRate.lastN.description: Successful ÷ completed runs over the last N. Repo-level.

ship.ci.flakyProxy.rate.label: CI instability proxy
ship.ci.flakyProxy.rate.description: Share of jobs that both passed AND failed across the sampled runs. A job-level instability proxy — NOT a test-level flaky rate. Repo-level.

ship.ci.artifacts.totalSize.label: CI artifacts (total)
ship.ci.artifacts.totalSize.description: Total size of non-expired workflow artifacts. Repo-level.

ship.deploy.avgDuration.label: Deploy duration (avg)
ship.deploy.avgDuration.description: Average deployment duration (GitHub Deployments API, else deploy-named Actions jobs). Repo-level; unavailable if neither exists.

change.pr.avgChangedFiles.label: PR changed files (avg)
change.pr.avgChangedFiles.description: Average files touched per merged PR — a proxy for change surface. Repo-level, mixes all chantiers; not a controlled per-variant scenario.

change.pr.avgAdditions.label: PR additions (avg)
change.pr.avgAdditions.description: Average lines added per merged PR. Repo-level (display only).

change.pr.avgDeletions.label: PR deletions (avg)
change.pr.avgDeletions.description: Average lines deleted per merged PR. Repo-level (display only).

change.pr.avgTimeToMerge.label: PR time to merge (avg)
change.pr.avgTimeToMerge.description: Average created→merged duration for merged PRs — a proxy for review/merge latency. Repo-level.

change.pr.avgReviewCount.label: PR reviews (avg)
change.pr.avgReviewCount.description: Average number of reviews per merged PR. Repo-level (display only).

flakyProxy.method: job-level instability proxy: a job that both passed and failed across the sampled runs. NOT a test-level flaky rate.
